abv: Torres Natureo Rosé De-Alcoholised Wine - Cockburns of Leith
gluten_free: Natureo Rosado is rosé wine with the alcohol removed, made from a coupage of Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon varieties produced by Bodegas Torres in Catalonia.Natureo Rosado is made like any other rosé wine. The grapes go through a 24-hour maceration to extract a light colour. Alcoholic fermentation takes place in stainless steel tanks at a controlled temperature for 15 to 20 days. The resulting wine then undergoes a process to remove the alcohol.Bodegas Torres is a well-known, hundred-year-old family winery that, as well as producing wines in different parts of the world and having an international presence, spends some of its time recovering native varieties and producing high quality alcohol-free wines so that those who can’t drink alcohol can still enjoy the essence of wine. These are accessible and very well made wines.

abv: Noughty Blanc Dealcoholised Chenin & Chardonnay - Cockburns of Leith
gluten_free: Noughty Blanc is a dealcoholised white wine from South Africa, made from Chenin Blanc and Chardonnay grapes. Notes of yellow apple, lime and chamomile are present on the nose, with aromas of honeysuckle, citrus fruit and jasmine on the palate. Elegant balance of acidity and fruitiness. Beautifully crafted Chardonnay/Chenin Still White Wine from South Africa. <0.5% ABVProduced using 55% Chenin Blanc and 45% Chardonnay grapes harvested from dry-land farmed vineyards in the Western Cape region. The vineyards are strategically planted on south-west facing slopes to capture cool breezes from the Atlantic Ocean and cold Benguela current. Deep soils from decomposed granite, clay rich, with good drainage.With 14 days fermentation at 14-15°C, the grapes and juice are handled reductively to prevent oxidation and lock in fruitiness. The wine is left on lees for 2 months to add complexity. The wine is then dealcoholized by way of a gently spun cone technology. This is done under vacuum and at low temperature to ensure that the wine retains its distinctive body, colour and flavour. Vintage: Non-VintageVarietal: 55% Chenin Blanc / 45% ChardonnayVineyards: The grapes are harvested from dry-land farmed vineyards in the Western Cape region, where the vines are not trellised and are planted as bush vines. The vineyards are strategically planted on south-west facing slopes to capture cool breezes from the Atlantic Ocean and cold Benguela current. Deep soils from decomposed granite, clay rich, with good drainage.Winemaking: With 14 days fermentation at 14-15°C, the grapes and juice are handled reductively to prevent oxidation and lock in fruitiness. The wine is left on lees for 2 months to add complexity. The wine is then dealcoholized by way of a gently spun cone technology. This is done under vacuum and at low temperature to ensure that the wine retains its distinctive body, colour and flavour.Pairing: Great alternative to any elegant alcoholic white wine to be paired accordingly.
